title: Take a psychedelic trip in the city without taking drugs
content: Hidden away on a quiet residential street in San Francisco, the art installations and immersive experiences at the Gregangelo Museum have been opening minds since the 1980s. One thing that always strikes me about books like Michael Pollan’s  *How to Change Your Mind (opens in new tab)* (opens in new tab)is the challenge of reporting on the psychedelic experience itself. And so it is during our afternoon in the sylvan SF neighborhood of St. Francis Woods. The Gregangelo Museum (opens in new tab) is an outlier on its block, a wildly re-imagined private home divided into a labyrinth of 27 spaces that feel like cinematic tableaux. In each of the rooms, we’ll be asked to reveal something about our present, past, and future. “This place sprouted from an old can of paint I found in the basement and a ripped-up shirt,” he says, “because I didn’t have a brush at the time.” But the museum’s present form began to take shape during the 1980s.

title: Press and Reviews - Psychedelic Sf Art Gallery
content: In conclusion, the San Francisco psychedelic art culture of the 1960s was a unique and powerful expression of the transformative power of visionary experiences.

title: San Francisco Gregangelo Museum - Immersive Interior Art ...
content: Embark on a psychedelic odyssey through nine curious installations, where Alice in Wonderland meets 1001 Arabian Nights in a whirlwind of art and imagination.
